title: Reconciliation residual diagnostic for the CPIS-augmented global SFC FWTW matrix
supersedes: prior dp2_residual.json (which (a) INVERTED the BIS claims/liabilities labels, (b) reported an unreconstructable $3.96tn row residual whose column-target vector was never persisted, and (c) recorded CPIS bilateral as a HOLE).
verifier: build/results/dp2_recompute_check.json (regenerates every residual below from ras_inputs.json alone)
method: M2_RAS_sinkhorn, named in matrix_spec.json before use.
headline_finding: CPIS now supplies 167 OBSERVED-bilateral portfolio cells (13 reporters x 14 counterparts), refuting the prior HOLE. Two residual layers are reported on their own terms and NOT conflated: (1) the CPIS portfolio block is margin-CONSISTENT (its RAS to its own margins converges to ~1e-7, an EXACT-BY-CONSTRUCTION consistency check, NOT an independent reconciliation, because CPIS has no independent portfolio-liability column marginal on disk); (2) the BIS banking-marginal FLOOR is $711,157.7mn and is IRREDUCIBLE PRE-RAS — same source, same 18 areas, claims and liabilities that do not net. CPIS densifies the portfolio layer; it does NOT touch the banking floor. The residual did NOT collapse to zero: it is dominated by this $711bn banking floor, which is unchanged by populating CPIS.

bis_marginal_asymmetry.LABEL_CORRECTION: Prior artifact INVERTED these. Corrected and VERIFIED against the BIS source: lbs_compact.json header states position_C='Total claims', position_L='Total liabilities'. Summing the on-disk USD vectors over the 18 named non-US areas gives position C = 9,529,422.9 (CLAIMS) and position L = 10,240,580.6 (LIABILITIES).
bis_marginal_asymmetry.bis_source_excerpt: build/data/bis_lbs/lbs_compact.json header: "position_C": "Total claims", "position_L": "Total liabilities", "unit": "USD millions". Each row carries denom/position/cp_country/value_usd_mn; e.g. {position:C, cp_country:CN, value_usd_mn:236443.208} is a CLAIM on China, {position:L, cp_country:CN, value_usd_mn:638801.0} a LIABILITY to China.
bis_marginal_asymmetry.denominator_basis_for_pct: Prior 7.19% matched no reproducible denominator. Stated explicitly here: 711,157.7 / 9,529,422.9 (claims base) = 7.463%; / 10,240,580.6 (liab base) = 6.945%; / mean = 7.194%. The unambiguous figure is the ABSOLUTE $711,157.7mn; the percentage is denominator-dependent and reported only with its base.
bis_marginal_asymmetry.us_self_position_excluded: US-on-US ($8,262,469.8 claims / $4,194,157.2 liab) excluded as a near-tautological self-position; this is what reconciles these totals to the audit's target figures.
bis_marginal_asymmetry.reading: This creditor/debtor asymmetry is itself an irreducible reconciliation residual: even before any RAS, marginals from the SAME source on the SAME areas do not net to zero. Any 'balanced' matrix sits at least $711bn from internal consistency. CPIS does not reduce it.

pass_1_cpis_portfolio_consistency.scope: CPIS area x area portfolio block, 13 reporters x 14 counterparts, 167 observed cells, 2025-S1, USD mn.
pass_1_cpis_portfolio_consistency.honest_label: CONSISTENCY pass, NOT an independent reconciliation. Targets are the block's OWN row/col sums. Converges by construction; verifies the seed is margin-consistent. It is NOT presented as reconciling the portfolio layer against an external source — no such independent portfolio-liability marginal exists on disk for these areas (asset-vs-derived-liability mirror remains an open CPIS friction).
pass_1_cpis_portfolio_consistency.interpretation: row_resid_L2 and col_resid_L2 -> ~1e-7 (machine-level). This confirms the assembled CPIS cells are self-consistent; it does NOT certify reconciliation against any independent marginal.

pass_2_bis_banking_marginal.scope: BIS LBS USD positions, 18 named non-US areas, 2025-Q4.
pass_2_bis_banking_marginal.honest_label: Independent reconciliation: claims (creditor-reported) vs liabilities (debtor-reported), same source, same areas. They do NOT net.
pass_2_bis_banking_marginal.is_irreducible: This floor exists BEFORE any RAS and is NOT reduced by adding CPIS portfolio cells (different instrument layer).

post_ras_residual_relative_to_floor.statement: The CPIS portfolio block's post-pass residual is ~7e-7 USD mn (machine-level, expected for a consistency pass). The BINDING residual for the assembled matrix is the BIS banking floor of $711,157.7mn, which is ~10^12 times larger and is UNTOUCHED by the CPIS layer. Reported relative to the floor: the portfolio block contributes negligibly to internal inconsistency; the $711bn banking-marginal asymmetry is where the matrix sits from internal consistency. We do NOT claim the residual collapsed — the floor is intact.
post_ras_residual_relative_to_floor.do_not_misread: The ~0 CPIS residual is NOT a claim that the global matrix is reconciled. It is a margin-consistency artifact of a single asset-side block balanced to its own margins.

largest_residuals_concentration.by_area_AND_instrument: Largest residuals concentrate in the BANKING (I1) layer at offshore/financial-center and China-channel areas. Per-area BIS liability-minus-claim (L - C, USD mn):
largest_residuals_concentration.where_they_sit: KY (Cayman, L-C = -457,472.6: claims exceed liabilities), CN (China, +402,357.8: liabilities exceed claims), HK (+322,507.8), CH (+233,989.8), CA (-230,512.4). The CHINA and OFFSHORE-HUB (KY, HK) areas dominate the asymmetry — exactly the residency/nationality channel. China's banking LIABILITIES exceed claims by $402bn, consistent with the direction of the residency understatement (offshore-booked Chinese exposure not captured as a direct China claim). Instrument: I1 deposits/loans (BIS). The CPIS portfolio layer (I2/I3) is internally consistent and does not add to this concentration.

gcap_residency_to_nationality.applied: The residency->nationality reallocation requires the COUNTERPARTY ISSUER's nationality. CPIS COUNTERPART_COUNTRY is RESIDENCY. The offshore-hub portfolio cells are therefore tagged residency-attributed and NOT reallocated; the China-by-nationality fraction is a BOUNDED HOLE.

offshore_pool_real_measured.note: $2,956,997mn of US-resident portfolio holdings booked to CYM+HKG+VGB residence. This pool is REAL and MEASURED; an unquantified fraction is Chinese-by-nationality.

effect_on_load_bearing_china_cell.us_to_cayman_dwarfs_us_to_china: US->CYM ($2,798,130mn) exceeds US->CHN ($255,341mn) by ~11x. The masked-China pool is VISIBLE and BOUNDED, not misattributed.
effect_on_load_bearing_china_cell.direction: upward (true China-by-nationality exposure exceeds the direct $255,341mn residency cell).
effect_on_load_bearing_china_cell.magnitude: HOLE — not computable from on-disk data. CPIS does NOT close the offshore-SPV issuer-nationality gap (COUNTERPART_COUNTRY is residency). bis_cbs narrows it for the bank-claims leg ONLY, not for portfolio. The fraction of the $2.96tn offshore portfolio pool that is Chinese-by-nationality is NOT obtainable; we do NOT invent it.

gcap_residency_to_nationality.explicit_non_assumption: The China residency understatement is NEITHER confirmed at a value NOR assumed negligible. It is a marked, bounded HOLE: the offshore pool it draws from is real ($2.96tn CPIS / $2.07tn BIS Cayman+HK), the China-attributed fraction is unquantified-by-absence-of-nationality-data.

ESTIMATED_cells.ras_consistency_scaled: the 167 CPIS cells are rescaled by <1e-6 in the consistency pass; they remain OBSERVED (no mass added/moved between sources). No uniform-prior cells were seeded this run.

UNVERIFIED_by_absence_blocks: non_us_non_ea_advanced_FWTW counterpart-sector (oecd: dataflow empty) — partially covered by CPIS JPN/GBR reporter rows on residency
HOLE_blocks: VGB reporter row (BVI files no CPIS; 13 cells)
176 CPIS non-reporting economies (no reporter rows; valid as counterparts only)
offshore-SPV issuer-NATIONALITY of the CYM/HKG/VGB portfolio pool (residency-vs-nationality gap; bounded)
safe_china_outward_portfolio_by_country (HTML/Excel; partly superseded by CPIS CHN reporter row)

census_delta_vs_prior_706.prior_cpis_status: HOLE (0 bilateral cells; recorded as one-dense-column-only)
census_delta_vs_prior_706.driver: +167 CPIS OBSERVED-bilateral portfolio cells (prior: 0). The other blocks (Fed 585, TIC 36, BIS 76, COFER 9) are unchanged. Net observed cell count rises from 706 to 873.
census_delta_vs_prior_706.load_bearing_cells_now_populated: USA->CHN, USA->CYM, USA->HKG, USA->VGB, USA->JPN, USA->GBR, JPN->USA, CHN->USA, CHN->* (China as reporter, 13 cells), CYM->*, and the full 13x14 off-diagonal grid except the 3 marked intra-block holes. The China channel (direct + offshore hubs) is fully populated on a residency basis with the nationality gap marked.
